b <- data.frame(id = 2:4, y = c("x", "y", "z"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
left_join(a, b, by = "id")
generated_code:
a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, x = c("a", "b", "c"))
b <- data.frame(id = 2:4, y = c("x", "y", "z"))
a
b
merge(a, b, by = "id", all.x = TRUE)

                                ifelse(mtcars$mpg >= 15, "medium", "low"))
head(mtcars[, c("mpg", "mpg_cat")], 10)
table(mtcars$mpg_cat)

nl: using mtcars, make a scatter plot of mpg vs wt and add a smoothed regression line
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
generated_code:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x) +
  labs(x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per Gallon",
       title = "MPG vs Weight (mtcars)") +
  theme_minimal()

nl: using mtcars, first compute the mean mpg for each cyl, then make a bar chart of those means with cyl as the x-axis and mean mpg as the bar height
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>% group_by(cyl) %>% summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg)) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mean_mpg)) + geom_col()
generated_code:
means <- aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean)
means
barplot(mpg ~ cyl, data = means, 
        xlab = "Cylinders", ylab = "Mean MPG",
        main = "Mean MPG by Cylinder",
        col = "steelblue")

nl: create a correlation matrix from mtcars using cor(), reshape it into a long format, then plot it as a heatmap tile chart
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
cor_mat <- cor(mtcars)
cor_long <- melt(cor_mat)
ggplot(cor_long, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) + geom_tile()
generated_code:
cor_matrix <- cor(mtcars)
cor_matrix[1:5, 1:5]
cor_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(cor_matrix))
names(cor_long) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "Correlation")
head(cor_long)
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(cor_long, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Correlation, 2)), size = 3) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of mtcars", x = "", y = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

nl: using mtcars with cyl as a factor, compute the mean and standard deviation of mpg for each cylinder count, then plot the means as points with error bars for the standard deviation
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>% group_by(cyl) %>% summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg), sd_mpg = sd(mpg)) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mean_mpg)) + geom_point() + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_mpg - sd_mpg, ymax = mean_mpg + sd_mpg), width = 0.2)
generated_code:
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

df <- mtcars %>%
  mutate(cyl = factor(cyl)) %>%
  group_by(cyl) %>%
  summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg), sd_mpg = sd(mpg), .groups = "drop")
df
ggplot(df, aes(x = cyl, y = mean_mpg)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_mpg - sd_mpg, ymax = mean_mpg + sd_mpg), width = 0.15) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean MPG by Cylinder Count",
    subtitle = "Error bars: ±1 standard deviation",
    x = "Cylinders",
    y = "MPG"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

nl: categorize the mpg values of mtcars into three equal-sized bins labeled low, medium, and high
generated_code:
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>%
  mutate(mpg_category = cut(mpg, breaks = 3, labels = c("low", "medium", "high"))) %>%
  select(mpg, mpg_category) %>%
  arrange(mpg)

mtcars %>%
  mutate(mpg_category = ntile(mpg, 3) %>%
           factor(labels = c("low", "medium", "high"))) %>%
  select(mpg, mpg_category)
